info.title: PLMKR — Playmaker API
info.description:
AI-powered artist management platform. Agents handle curator pitching, PR outreach, venue booking, social scheduling, and weekly reporting — no human copy-paste required.

**Phases:**
- Phase 1: Gmail OAuth + Curator pitch lifecycle
- Phase 2: PR contacts + Booking inquiries
- Phase 3: Social post scheduling + Weekly reports

All endpoints require an `artist_id` (the artist's unique identifier in the system).

get.description: Returns 200 OK when the service is running.
Redirect artist to Google OAuth consent screen.
Exchange OAuth code for tokens and persist in artist profile.
Return whether artist has active Gmail tokens.

post.description: Send a one-off email via artist's connected Gmail account.
Load curators from data/curators_seed.json. Idempotent.
Generate (but do not send) a pitch draft for one curator.

For each curator_id:
  1. generate_pitch_email()  → draft
  2. _db_create_pitch()      → status=draft
  3. send_email()            → status=sent
Returns {"sent": N, "failed": M, "errors": [...], "pitch_ids": [...]}.
Manually trigger inbox scan for one artist.

Find sent pitches that hit a follow-up threshold today,
generate follow-up emails, and send them.
Returns {"queued": N, "sent": M, "failed": K, "details": [...]}.

For each contact: generate PR email, save outreach record (draft),
send via Gmail, update status to sent.
Returns {"sent": N, "failed": M, "errors": [...], "outreach_ids": [...]}.
Manually trigger PR inbox scan for one artist.

Find sent PR outreach on day 3 or 7, generate follow-ups, send them.
Returns {"queued": N, "sent": M, "failed": K, "details": [...]}.

For each contact: generate booking email, save inquiry record (draft),
send via Gmail, update status to sent.
Returns {"sent": N, "failed": M, "errors": [...], "inquiry_ids": [...]}.
Manually trigger booking inbox scan for one artist.

Single Gmail auth, then run pitch + PR + booking reply detection in sequence.
One Gmail API authentication round-trip per call instead of three.

Find sent booking inquiries on day 5 or 14, generate follow-ups, send them.
Returns {"queued": N, "sent": M, "failed": K, "details": [...]}.

get.description: Redirect artist to Buffer OAuth consent screen.
Handle Buffer OAuth callback — exchange code for access token and store it.

post.description:
Generate req.posts_per_platform posts for each platform.
Space them evenly across the week starting from start_date.
Optionally schedule via Buffer (mocked).
Returns {"generated": N, "scheduled_via_buffer": M, "errors": [...], "post_ids": [...]}.

get.description:
Return activity stats for an artist.
since: ISO datetime string (optional). If omitted, returns all-time stats.
Per-model Anthropic call counters (total, success, retry, fail). Auth required.
Per-artist Gmail call counters (total, success, retry, fail). Auth required.

Per-route p50/p95/p99 latency percentiles. Auth required.
Rolling window of last 1000 requests per route.
Full runtime diagnostics. Requires X-API-Key. Never exposes env var values.

Readiness check: DB, scheduler, OAuth token counts, disk usage, and
security posture of the running deploy.

Returns 503 when db_connected=False so Railway restarts on DB failure.

post.description: Create a new release.

get.description: List all releases for an artist.

post.description:
Generate campaign_actions for a release. Idempotent — clears existing
pending actions and regenerates from the current release_date.

get.description: List all campaign actions for a release.

post.description:
Execute all campaign actions for this release that are due (scheduled_for <= now).
Updates action status to done/failed with result.

Returns the agent's opening line when an artist enters a chat.

ALL agents now use static rotating greetings — zero API calls.
This was the single biggest source of unnecessary token spend during testing.
Each agent has 3–5 handcrafted variants; Marcus has 5.

New agent delivers a warm personalised greeting after Marcus routes to them.

Receiving agent gets full conversation context (HISTORY_CAP_HANDOFF turns)
so they can greet the artist by name and reference what was discussed.
The from_agent_id param makes the handoff prompt dynamic (not hardcoded to Marcus).

The greeting prompt now instructs the agent to use the artist's name and
reference something specific from the recent conversation. This fixes the
generic "I'm routing you to X" wording from the previous version.
Mark a call as ended so any in-flight /api/tts/synth for that call returns null.
Synthesize text → base64 WAV. Used by app to bypass SSE buffering.

get.description: Returns whether TTS is ready. True if Kokoro loaded OR ElevenLabs key present.
Find existing artist profile by name (case-insensitive) across all artist profiles.

post.description: Send a 6-digit OTP via Twilio SMS. Requires TWILIO_* env vars.
Verify a 6-digit OTP. Consumes the code on success.
Save Expo push token to the artist's JSON file.
Send a push notification to an artist via Expo push API and store in history.

get.description: Return the artist's notification history from their JSON file.
Check if D-ID avatar feature is available.
